scope.selection_note: Codex-only bounded prep artifact. Used deterministic local inspection and the exported validateRepo helper directly; did not run the validate CLI.

validator_evidence: duplicate_global_identity: Global corridor records corridor_14th-st-crosstown-brt-phase2 and corridor_14th-street-ttp share identity key corridor_14th-st-crosstown-brt-phase2.
duplicate_global_identity: Global corridor records corridor_14th-st-crosstown-brt-phase2 and corridor_14th-street-ttp share identity key corridor_14th-st-crosstown-corridor.
source_canonical_evidence: corridor_14th-st-crosstown-brt-phase2: Manhattan; display_name/corridor_name '14th Street Crosstown Corridor'; BRT Phase II candidate corridor; source brt_phase2_future_corridors_rpt#p004_c0002 lists '14th Street Crosstown Corridor' in the Phase II candidates table.

corridor_14th-street-ttp: Manhattan; street '14th Street'; limits '3rd Avenue to 8th Avenue (westbound) and 9th Avenue to 3rd Avenue (eastbound)'; merged corridor_name values include '14th Street Crosstown Corridor'; source 14th_street_busway_brochure#p001_c0002 says 14th Street between 9th Avenue and 3rd Avenue would become a Transit & Truck Priority corridor.

corridor_14th-street-ttp also carries brt_intro_to_phase2 source context with raw_text '14th Street Crosstown Corridor - Difficult Trip - Existing M14 bus route carries 39,000 trips a day', tying the Phase II corridor surface to the same Manhattan 14th Street corridor identity.
proposed_aliases.reason: The Phase II record is a sparse planning-era surface for the same 14th Street crosstown corridor later materialized as the 14th Street TTP/busway corridor. This is a corridor lifecycle/name surface, not a conflicting borough or limit split.

validator_evidence: duplicate_global_identity: Global corridor records corridor_woodhaven-blvd-brt-phase2 and corridor_woodhaven-boulevard share identity key corridor_woodhaven-blvd-corridor.
source_canonical_evidence: corridor_woodhaven-blvd-brt-phase2: Queens; display_name/corridor_name 'Woodhaven Blvd Corridor'; BRT Phase II candidate corridor; source brt_phase2_future_corridors_rpt#p004_c0002 lists 'Woodhaven Blvd Corridor' in the Phase II candidates table.

corridor_woodhaven-boulevard: Queens; corridor_name 'Woodhaven Boulevard'; current payload limits 'Queens Boulevard to 107th Avenue'; record_aliases include 'corridor_woodhaven-blvd-corridor', 'corridor_woodhaven-blvd-q52-q53-sbs-corridor', and 2015 Woodhaven SBS surfaces.

corridor_woodhaven-boulevard source evidence includes better_buses#p001_b0001 for 'Woodhaven Boulevard in Queens, from Queens Boulevard to 107th Avenue' and 2015_04_01_brt_woodhaven_newsletter_1_spring2015#p002_c0004 for 'Woodhaven Blvd - Main Road Bus Lanes'.
proposed_aliases.reason: The Phase II record is a minimal planning-candidate shell for Woodhaven Boulevard, while the canonical Woodhaven Boulevard record already aggregates the same Woodhaven Blvd corridor surface and later Q52/Q53 SBS/Better Buses evidence. No conflicting borough or separate street limit is present in the Phase II shell.

validator_evidence: duplicate_global_identity: Global corridor records corridor_bus-fwd-2-church-av-brooklyn-local and corridor_church-av share identity key corridor_church-av.
source_canonical_evidence: corridor_bus-fwd-2-church-av-brooklyn-local: Brooklyn; corridor_name 'Church Av'; from Coney Island Av to Flatbush Av; source bus_forward_2#p011_c0002 lists 'Church Av from Coney Island Av to Flatbush Av' under local bus-priority corridors.

corridor_church-av: Brooklyn; corridor_name 'Church Av'; limits 'Flatbush Ave to Ocean Pkwy'; source church_ave_cb12_jun2019#p006_c0002 states 'Flatbush Ave to Ocean Pkwy (0.9 miles)' and splits that corridor into Flatbush Ave to E 16 St and E 16 St to Ocean Pkwy.
The two records touch at Flatbush Avenue but cover different Church Avenue sections.
proposed_do_not_merge_pairs.reason: Shared Church Avenue street identity hides adjacent but distinct corridor limits: Coney Island Avenue to Flatbush Avenue versus Flatbush Avenue to Ocean Parkway. The pair should be guarded as DNM rather than collapsed.

validator_evidence: duplicate_global_identity: Global corridor records corridor_cross-bay-blvd-2015 and corridor_cross-bay-blvd-sbs-spring2015 share identity key corridor_cross-bay-blvd.
source_canonical_evidence: corridor_cross-bay-blvd-2015: Queens; corridor_name 'Cross Bay Boulevard (north of 165 Av)'; limits 'north of 165 Av'; source 2015_05_13_brt_woodhaven_cb6_presentation#p020_c0009 states 'Cross Bay Blvd (north of 165 Av)' with offset bus lanes and bus bulbs.

corridor_cross-bay-blvd-sbs-spring2015: Queens; corridor_name 'Cross Bay Boulevard'; merged description includes 'Cross Bay Boulevard bus lanes between 107 Av and N Conduit Av'; source brt_woodhaven_implementation_update_2017#p002_c0006 states 'Cross Bay Blvd bus lanes between 107 Av and N Conduit Av'.
Existing guardrails already distinguish corridor_cross-bay-blvd-2015 from other Cross Bay/whole Woodhaven-Cross Bay extents; this pair is another shared-street but different-limit case.
proposed_do_not_merge_pairs.reason: The records share Cross Bay Boulevard but carry different physical extents: a north-of-165-Av design segment versus the later 107 Av to N Conduit Av bus-lane segment. Shared street name is not enough for corridor identity.

validator_evidence: duplicate_global_identity: Global corridor records corridor_125th-st-crosstown-brt-phase2 and corridor_125th-st-manhattan share identity key corridor_125th-st-crosstown-corridor.
deferred.reason: Deferred for cluster-level corridor policy review. Both are Manhattan 125th Street crosstown surfaces, but one is only a Phase II candidate shell and the other has concrete Morningside Avenue to Second Avenue and other merged 125th Street limits. This was already deferred in batch203 and remains below high-confidence alias/DNM threshold.

validator_evidence: duplicate_global_identity: Global corridor records corridor_bus-fwd-2-church-av-brooklyn-sbs-candidate and corridor_church-av share identity key corridor_church-ave.
deferred.reason: Deferred because the Bus Forward 2 SBS candidate is a sparse 'Church Avenue' next-phase candidate without explicit limits, while corridor_church-av is the later Flatbush Avenue to Ocean Parkway priority corridor. No strong limit conflict or exact-limit match is present.

validator_evidence: duplicate_global_identity: Global corridor records corridor_utica-ave-b46-2015-04-06 and corridor_utica-ave-brt-phase2 share identity key corridor_utica-ave-corridor.

duplicate_global_identity: Global corridor records corridor_utica-ave-b46-2015-04-06 and corridor_utica-ave-st-johns-pl-church-ave-2013 share identity key corridor_utica-ave-b46-corridor.
duplicate_global_identity: Global corridor records corridor_utica-ave-brt-phase2 and corridor_utica-ave-st-johns-pl-church-ave-2013 share identity key corridor_utica-ave-brt-phase2.
duplicate_global_identity: Global corridor records corridor_utica-ave-b46-2015-04-06 and corridor_utica-ave-st-johns-pl-church-ave-2013 share identity key corridor_utica-ave-corridor.
deferred.reason: Deferred for Utica Avenue cluster review. The current records mix broad B46/Phase II corridor surfaces with bounded St. Johns to Carroll and St. Johns/Carroll to Church segments, and existing aliases already point some Utica surfaces through multi-step targets. Pairwise alias or DNM would be possible to overstate without a cluster-level split/merge policy call.
